chapter_title.ar: باب الْمَوَاقِيتِ أَوَّلِهَا وَ آخِرِهَا وَ أَفْضَلِهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 4 – The timings – its beginning, and its ending, and the best of it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/29)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/29)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) لَفَضْلُ الْوَقْتِ الْأَوَّلِ عَلَى الْأَخِيرِ خَيْرٌ لِلرَّجُلِ مِنْ وَلَدِهِ وَ مَالِهِ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Ahmad Bin Is’haq, from Bakr Bin Muhammad Al Azdy who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Preferring the beginning time upon the last is better for the man than his children and his wealth’.
en.sarwar: 7. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Ishaq from Bakr ibn Muhammad al-Azdiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The outstanding quality of the beginning of time for Salah (prayer) compared with the end of time for Salah (prayer) is better for a man than his children and belongings.’”
